section.name: secret-rotation
section.summary: Rotation schedule by secret type, safe rotation procedure, and 1Password op inject workflow for zero-secret-on-disk deployments.

rule.statement: Always generate the new secret before revoking the old one; never create a gap where neither works.
rule.rationale: Revoking first leaves services broken until the new secret is deployed. Generating first allows a brief overlap window where both are valid, enabling zero-downtime rotation.

rule.statement: Restart all affected services immediately after updating .env; stale processes still hold the old secret in memory.
rule.rationale: A running service loaded its environment variables at startup. Updating .env on disk has no effect on running processes until they are restarted and reload the file via systemd EnvironmentFile.

list.item: API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI): rotate every 90 days or immediately on suspected compromise
Database passwords: rotate every 180 days or on team member departure
JWT signing keys: rotate every 90 days (invalidates all active sessions)
SSH keys: rotate annually or on machine compromise

example.code:
# Safe rotation procedure (zero-downtime) # 1. Generate new secret in provider dashboard (keep old active) # 2. Update in 1Password vault op item edit "Anthropic" "api-key=sk-ant-api03-NEW_VALUE"
# 3. Regenerate .env from 1Password template op inject -i ~/workspace/.env.tpl -o ~/workspace/.env chmod 600 ~/workspace/.env
# 4. Restart affected services systemctl --user restart nero-core nero-channel-web nero-channel-tg
# 5. Verify services healthy systemctl --user status nero-core curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8100/health
# 6. Revoke old secret in provider dashboard # 7. Done — old secret is now invalid but services run on new one

section.name: op-inject-workflow
section.summary: 1Password op inject pattern: .env.tpl references vault items; op inject generates .env at deploy time with no secrets on disk between rotations.

rule.statement: Store the OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN in ~/.bashrc or a secure environment (not in .env.tpl itself); it is the master key that unlocks all other secrets.
rule.rationale: The service account token is a bootstrap credential that cannot be stored in the vault it unlocks. It belongs in a separate secret storage path (system environment or secure config).

section.reference: Python startup validator: required vars list, fail-fast with clear error message
.env.tpl with 1Password op:// references for common service credentials
